title: Errors, Cleanup, and Root-Cause Debugging · Retrieval Circuit
summary: Diagnose malformed records and failed operations while keeping cleanup, user recovery, and causal evidence intact. Learners retrieve rules and procedures before seeing feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a recoverable import and diagnostics workflow after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Differentiate syntax, reference, type, range, aggregate, DOM, and domain errors by evidence and recovery boundary.
Throw Error objects with stable messages, structured context, and preserved causes without exposing secrets.
Place try, catch, and finally around the smallest recoverable boundary without swallowing failures or overriding control flow.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.javascript:
// javascript-basics cumulative stakeholder program.
// Add one runnable behavior, assertion, changed case, and evidence return per activity.

steps.title: Retrieve the complete evidence cycle
steps.instruction: Order the Error Types evidence cycle for this review task before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Error Types assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

content.text: Import 6 rows containing one malformed record and one simulated storage failure. Preserve the original error cause, clean up resources, recover only the expected row failure, and let programming defects surface.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Error Types has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

hints: Name the Error Types precondition or invariant that the review case must preserve.

Trace this concrete case before choosing: Import 6 rows containing one malformed record and one simulated storage failure. Preserve the original error cause, clean up resources, recover only the expected row failure, and let programming defects surface.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: Every JavaScript failure is a generic Error with equivalent meaning.

options.text: Declare inputs, observable output, ownership, and the runtime behavior being tested
Trace values, bindings, receivers, mutations, and queued work before editing code
Implement the smallest behavior and retain an assertion over the actual result
Run boundary and changed-event cases, inspect failures, and remove accidental coupling

stimulus.title: Resource Cleanup case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: Import 8 rows containing one malformed record and one simulated storage failure. Preserve the original error cause, clean up resources, recover only the expected row failure, and let programming defects surface.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: Garbage collection automatically cancels all external resources.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Release listeners, timers, streams, locks, and disposable resources on success, failure, and cancellation. Artifact under review: a recoverable import and diagnostics workflow.

options.text: The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.
The record names the assumption, applies Resource Cleanup, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

steps.title: Record what required effort

Preserve the original error cause, clean up resources, recover only the expected row failure, and let programming defects surface.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Release listeners, timers, streams, locks, and disposable resources on success, failure, and cancellation.
